element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Immediate mechanic in town before combat
Vertical navigation in towns and dungeons

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Opening sequence - Elven king grants 1500 gold

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Strategic load-out decisions before dungeon entry
Long-term progression and Guru rank updates
Alternative damage source with magic bar cost
Save progression via mantras; rank bestowal

element_introduction_order.element: Enemy item drops (bread, gold, special items)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Resource supplementation and occasional special effects
Temporary power-ups tracked in HUD time display
Key acquisition and selective access gating

element_introduction_order.element: World Tree progression (buttress → trunk → branches → mountain)
Dwarf King Grieve and Dragon Slayer plot
The Evil One and meteorite lore

typical_relief_triggers: Visiting churches for rank-up and password saves
typical_tension_triggers: Low health state without healing items
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Oscillating safe-town exploration and dungeon delving. Towns allow strategic purchasing and guru dialogue for narrative/mechanical understanding. Dungeons escalate in enemy density and hazard complexity. Boss encounters punctuate each major world area. Password system breaks rhythm artificially for western audiences unfamiliar with the mechanic.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Branching paths within dungeons and optional shop purchases; exploration off main sequence encouraged by treasure drops and dialogue hints
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Unknown from sources; likely hidden rooms or alternative pathways (unused alternate final dungeon entrance exists in code)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction in Eolis with basic enemies, ample gold, and safe town navigation. Ladder climbing and platforming mechanics taught organically before combat escalation.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Tree interior and branch areas introduce poisoned water hazards, multiple enemy types per screen, and dungeon complexity. Equipment upgrades become strategic necessity. Guru rank-ups provide stat boosts to match escalating threats.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Dwarves' mountain stronghold with densest enemy encounters. Boss fights require both equipment and magic proficiency. Dragon Slayer acquisition (from Dwarf King Grieve) gated by progression mastery. Final confrontation with The Evil One demands full mastery of combat, magic, and resource management.
notable_spikes: First dungeon transition from safe town exploration
Introduction of poisoned water mechanic and hazard avoidance
Final Dwarf King Grieve fight spanning multiple screens (original design intent)

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Town Hub with Shop and Church
notable_level_patterns.description: Safe zones for resource management, dialogue-based exposition, and password save system. Strategic pause before dungeon entry. Multiple towns (Eolis, Dartmoor implied) provide equipment tier progression.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Eolis (starting town) and subsequent regional hubs

notable_level_patterns.description: Enemies appear in discrete room layouts. Player navigates platforming challenges between encounters. Ladder climbing and vertical space utilization. Keys gate progression; backtracking sometimes required for missed items.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tree buttress, tree trunk, tree branches dungeons

notable_level_patterns.description: Poisoned water areas introduce damage-on-contact mechanics requiring careful platforming. Hazard avoidance parallels combat challenge.
Culmination of each world area. Multi-screen or single-screen design. Requires synthesized mastery of combat, magic, and equipment. Drops significant rewards and progression gates.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Dwarf King Grieve final encounter (originally multi-screen design)

notable_level_patterns.description: Four-world structure progressing spatially through World Tree: buttress (roots) → trunk (interior) → branches (canopy) → mountain (stronghold beyond). Each world thematically distinct with escalating enemy types and hazards.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Overworld structure spanning all four regions
